articles.title: Astronauts Fly to the Moon on Artemis II Mission
articles.summary: Four astronauts are on a big trip to the Moon in a spacecraft called Orion. They left Earth's orbit after their engine fired for almost six minutes. This engine burn, called translunar injection, worked perfectly. The astronauts are feeling good and excited. They are going around the far side of the Moon and back, which is the first time humans have gone beyond Earth's orbit since 1972. From space, Earth looks smaller as they move away. The crew thanked everyone who helped make this mission happen. They will travel farther from Earth than anyone before, maybe breaking a record. Even after the engine burn, they can still turn back to Earth if needed. This mission shows what people can achieve together.

articles.title: A Mother's Fight for Justice After a Funeral Mistake
articles.summary: Jasmine Beverley is a mother who lost her baby boy, Sunny, before he was born. A funeral director named Robert Bush handled Sunny's funeral but made a big mistake. He gave families the wrong ashes and kept bodies at his place for too long. Jasmine got Sunny's ashes in the same box she had used for him, which made her suspicious. Later, police found Sunny's remains at Bush's funeral home. Jasmine was very sad and worried during her next pregnancy. Bush admitted to doing wrong things in court. Now, Jasmine wants to change the law so this doesn't happen to other families. She believes Bush will face consequences for his actions.

articles.title: M&S Boss Wants Help to Stop Store Crime
articles.summary: The boss of Marks & Spencer, a big store, is asking for more help to stop crime in shops. He says that workers are facing bad behavior like stealing and violence every day. Recently, there have been many problems, such as gangs breaking into stores, people taking things without paying, and workers getting hurt. In one place in London, a large group of young people caused trouble near an M&S store. The boss wrote letters to important leaders, like the London Mayor and a government minister, to ask for more police support. He believes that crime is getting worse and needs everyone to work together to fix it. The store has spent a lot of money on security, but it is not enough without police help. Leaders agree that shoplifting is wrong and are trying to use data and new rules to catch criminals.

articles.title: Astronauts Talk from Space on Their Moon Trip
articles.summary: The Artemis II spacecraft is flying to the Moon with four astronauts on board. They shared their thoughts about the mission and what they have learned in space. Victor Glover, the pilot, said that from space, Earth looks beautiful and everyone looks like one people. This mission helps us learn more about space and working together. The astronauts are excited to explore the far side of the Moon. They hope to inspire others with their journey.

articles.title: A Leader Leaves the Justice Department
articles.summary: President Trump has asked Attorney General Pam Bondi to leave her job after 14 months. Bondi was in charge of the Justice Department, which helps make sure laws are followed. She faced criticism for how she handled a case about Jeffrey Epstein, a man who did bad things. The president was also unhappy because she did not prosecute people he sees as enemies. Bondi said her time as Attorney General was a big honor. Now, people are wondering what will happen next at the Justice Department. Ali Rogin talked to Mary McCord to learn more about the future.

articles.title: A Big Decision About a White House Ballroom
articles.summary: A group called the National Capital Planning Commission said yes to President Trump's plan to build a ballroom at the White House. This group makes sure buildings in Washington, D.C., are okay to build. They think the ballroom is needed and will look nice. But a judge said they must wait because Congress needs to agree too. In other news, the Army's top leader, General Randy George, is leaving his job right away because the Defense Secretary asked him to. Also, Democratic leaders are going to court to stop President Trump from changing rules about mail-in voting. They say he doesn't have the power to do that. In Colorado, a court said Tina Peters, who lied about elections, should get a new sentence because her free speech was hurt.
